description_long: This is a tall deciduous tree with a straight, upright trunk and a moderately dense canopy of green, broad leaves. The trunk is relatively slender at the base, with several primary branches extending outward and upward in an alternate pattern. The foliage is distributed throughout the upper two-thirds of the tree, allowing some light to filter through the canopy, and the leaves appear to be simple and flat. The bark is textured and grayish-brown, while the branches are thinner and more flexible than the trunk. No visible roots extend above the ground, and the overall silhouette is columnar with a slight asymmetry in branch distribution. This tree would be commonly found lining urban streets, in parks, or as part of landscaped green spaces.
description: A tall, upright deciduous tree with a moderately dense canopy of green broad leaves and a slender, textured trunk. The branches extend outward in an alternate pattern, creating a columnar silhouette.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height and straightness of the trunk, with the canopy appearing symmetrical and moderately dense.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the branching structure, revealing more of the tree's asymmetry and the spread of the foliage.
description_view_2: Rear view is similar to the front, showing the vertical trunk and the even distribution of leaves, with some gaps in the canopy.
description_view_3: This side view emphasizes the alternate branching and the way the foliage extends outward, with more visible gaps between branches.
